name: Brisket and Beans/cp or Pressure Cooker
recipeInstructions:
1. Wipe the brisket clean and place it in a large pot. Cover with about 2 cups of water.
Vegetables may be added to the meat in a slow cooker along with seasonings and cooked for 10 hours.
2. In a Dutch oven, simmer the meat for 2 to 2 1/2 hours before adding vegetables. Continue cooking until potatoes are tender.
3. In a pressure cooker, bring the brisket, water, and seasonings to 15 PSI pressure. Cook for 30 to 40 minutes.
Reduce pressure under cold water, open the cooker, add vegetables, cover, bring to 15 PSI pressure, and cook for 5 minutes.
Reduce pressure again.
Remove the meat, slice thinly on the diagonal, and serve with green beans and potatoes drenched in cooking liquid. Do not thicken the natural gravy. It should have a lovely flavor and should not be greasy. If your meat is not well trimmed, remove excess fat before cooking. Leftovers can be reheated by heating the remaining liquid and slicing the meat for open-faced or French dip sandwiches.
